summary: Joel Spolsky shares a brief update about upcoming speaking events in March 2005 and beyond. He mentions talks at O'Reilly Emerging Technology Conference, Software Development West, and a publisher meet-and-greet. He teases that his O'Reilly talk will explore why certain products and people become popular while comparable alternatives don't, using Brad Pitt vs. Ian Somerhalder and iPod vs. Creative Nomad as examples. The post also mentions a future keynote at a ColdFusion conference.
key_points: Joel was exploring the concept of why certain products achieve massive popularity while technically comparable alternatives fail
He was actively on the tech conference circuit in early 2005, speaking at major industry events
His talk premise—comparing celebrity appeal to product appeal—shows his characteristic approach of using pop culture analogies for tech insights
money_quotes: It will be somewhat similar to my talk at Amazon.com, which was about why Brad Pitt and the Apple iPod are so doggone popular, while Ian Somerhalder and the elegantly named "Creative Nomad Jukebox Zen Extra" are not.
Also there will be jokes and music.
I'm not sure where the fire is coming from.
key_insight: Product success often mirrors celebrity appeal—some products capture the public imagination while technically equivalent competitors languish in obscurity.
